title: lathyrus palustris f. pilosus
intro: Lathyrus palustris f. pilosus, also known as Marsh Pea or Marsh Vetchling, is a perennial legume native to wetlands and marshy areas in Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat urinary and kidney issues, as well as its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Lathyrus palustris f. pilosus include relief from kidney stones, bladder problems, and menstrual cramps, making it a valuable herb for women's health. Traditionally, Lathyrus palustris f. pilosus is prepared as a tea or tincture by infusing the roots and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, allowing for easy consumption of its medicinal properties.
